revision.comment: 新規作成
revision.text:
{{画像提供依頼|本殿や拝殿など、神社の様子がわかる写真|date=2019年7月}} {{表記揺れ案内|表記1=豊鹿嶋神社|表記2=豊鹿島神社|表記3=|議論ページ=}} {{神社 |名称=豊鹿嶋神社 |画像={{画像募集中}} |所在地=[[東京都]][[東大和市]]芋窪1-2067 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|45|28.5|N|139|24|46.2|E|scale:30000|display=title}} |祭神=武御加豆智命 |社格=[[郷社]] |創建=1466年 |例祭= |地図=Japan Tokyo|緯度度=35|緯度分=45|緯度秒=28.5|経度度=139|経度分=24|経度秒=46.2}} '''豊鹿嶋神社'''（とよかしまじんじゃ）とは、[[東京都]][[東大和市]]芋窪にある[[神社]]。祭神は'''武御加豆智命'''（たてみかつちのみこと）<ref name="cho">{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/higashiyamato/5762/|title=豊鹿嶋神社|publisher=[[神社庁|東京都神社庁]]|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>。その本殿は[[1466年]]（[[文正]]元年）建立であり、'''現存する[[東京都]]最古の神社建築'''である<ref name="cho"></ref>。
== 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[707年]]（[[慶雲]]4年）、[[武蔵国]]に来た鬼神を常陸峯に鎮めて、[[天智天皇]]の第四の姫宮と[[蘇我倉山田石川麻呂|蘇我山田石川麿]]が建立したことに始まる<ref name="book">{{Cite book |和書 |author=[[植田孟縉]]|year=1993|title=武蔵名勝図会 |page= |publisher=慶友社|location= |isbn=4874490808}}</ref>。
[[江戸時代]]には13石の[[社領]]を持っていた<ref name="city">{{Cite web|title=11 豊鹿島神社|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,5862,360,622,html|publisher=東大和市|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>。
[[1993年]]（[[平成]]5年）に行われた[[拝殿]]の大改修において、『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に記載されていた通り、1466年[[10月3日]]の[[棟札]]が発見され、創建年が実証された。また、この改修で計5枚の棟札が発見され、改修前の拝殿は[[1858年]]（[[安政]]5年）に完成したことが確認された。<ref name="book"></ref>
== 文化財 == 神社全体が、東京都の[[指定文化財]]に指定されている<ref name="city"></ref>。また、木製の狛犬と獅子頭が東大和市の指定文化財に指定されている<ref name="city"></ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[東京都の神社一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:とよかしましんしや}} [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:東大和市の建築物]]

revision.comment: 加筆。
revision.text:
{{画像提供依頼|本殿や拝殿など、神社の様子がわかる写真|cat=東大和市|date=2019年7月}} {{表記揺れ案内|表記1=豊鹿嶋神社|表記2=豊鹿島神社|表記3=|議論ページ=}} {{神社 |名称=豊鹿嶋神社 |画像={{画像募集中}} |所在地=[[東京都]][[東大和市]]芋窪1-2067 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|45|28.5|N|139|24|46.2|E|scale:30000|display=title}} |祭神=武御加豆智命 |社格=[[郷社]] |創建=1466年 |例祭= |地図=Japan Tokyo|緯度度=35|緯度分=45|緯度秒=28.5|経度度=139|経度分=24|経度秒=46.2}} '''豊鹿嶋神社'''（とよかしまじんじゃ）とは、[[東京都]][[東大和市]]芋窪にある[[神社]]。祭神は[[タケミカヅチ|武御加豆智命]]<ref name="cho">{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/higashiyamato/5762/|title=豊鹿嶋神社|publisher=[[神社庁|東京都神社庁]]|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>。本殿は[[室町時代]]の[[1466年]]（[[文正]]元年）建立であり、現存する東京都最古の神社建築である<ref name="cho"/>。
== 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[707年]]（[[慶雲]]4年）、[[武蔵国]]に来た鬼神を常陸峯に鎮めて、[[天智天皇]]の第四の姫宮と[[蘇我倉山田石川麻呂|蘇我山田石川麿]]が建立したことに始まる<ref name="book">{{Cite book |和書 |author=[[植田孟縉]]|year=1993|title=武蔵名勝図会 |page= |publisher=慶友社|location= |isbn=4874490808}}</ref>。以来、芋窪地域の鎮守として住民により守られてきた<ref name="cho"/>。
[[江戸時代]]には13石の[[社領]]を与えていた<ref name="city">{{Cite web|title=11 豊鹿島神社|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,5862,360,622,html|publisher=東大和市|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>。
[[1993年]]（[[平成]]5年）に行われた[[拝殿]]の大改修において、『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に記載されていた通り、1466年[[10月3日]]の[[棟札]]が発見され、創建年が実証された。また、この改修で計5枚の棟札が発見され、改修前の拝殿は[[1858年]]（[[安政]]5年）に完成したことが確認された<ref name="book"/>。
== 文化財 == ; 東京都指定有形文化財（建造物） * 豊鹿島神社本殿 1棟 附・棟札 1枚 - 1964年（昭和39年）11月21日に都重宝として指定、1976年（昭和51年）7月1日に種別変更<ref name="to">{{Cite web|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=205|title=豊鹿島神社本殿|website=東京都文化財情報データベース|publisher=[[東京都教育委員会|東京都教育庁]]地域教育支援部|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : [[流造|一間社流造]]の本殿で[[こけら葺]]箱棟付、桁行（間口）は170.7cm、身舎の梁間（奥行）は152.4cm、向拝の梁間は140.4cmである<ref name="to"/>。現状は外気や風雨から保護するための[[覆堂|覆殿]]の中にあり、外から観察することはできない<ref name="cho"/>。建築年代について、かつては本殿内に保管されていた棟札にあった年紀通りに1550年（[[天文 (元号)|天文]]19年）とされてきたが、1992年（平成4年）から2年に及ぶ解体修理の際、大工二郎三郎近吉が1466年（文正元年）に建立した旨を記した棟札が新たに発見され、また、古くは厚板葺の屋根であったことも分かった<ref name="to"/>。
; 東大和市指定文化財（市重宝） * 豊鹿島神社本殿の木製狛犬 - 1983年（昭和58年）3月1日指定<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|title=東大和市の文化財|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,2279,359,html|publisher=東大和市|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : 木製、本殿と同時期に作られた可能性が高いとされる<ref name="bunka"/>。 * 豊鹿島神社の獅子頭 - 1983年（昭和58年）3月1日指定<ref name="bunka"/> : 江戸時代後期の作か、かつて奉納されていた[[獅子舞]]に用いられたとみられる<ref name="bunka"/>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[東京都の神社一覧]] * [[東京都指定文化財一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:とよかしましんしや}} [[Category:鹿島神社]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:東京都指定有形文化財]] [[Category:東京都内の区市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:東大和市の建築物]] [[Category:東大和市の歴史]]

revision.comment: 画像追加
revision.text:
{{表記揺れ案内|表記1=豊鹿嶋神社|表記2=豊鹿島神社|表記3=|議論ページ=}} {{神社 |名称=豊鹿嶋神社 |画像=[[File:豊鹿嶋神社正面より.jpg|300px]] |所在地=[[東京都]][[東大和市]]芋窪1-2067 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|45|28.5|N|139|24|46.2|E|scale:30000|display=title}} |祭神=武御加豆智命 |社格=[[郷社]] |創建=1466年 |例祭= |地図=Japan Tokyo|緯度度=35|緯度分=45|緯度秒=28.5|経度度=139|経度分=24|経度秒=46.2}} '''豊鹿嶋神社'''（とよかしまじんじゃ）とは、[[東京都]][[東大和市]]芋窪にある[[神社]]。祭神は[[タケミカヅチ|武御加豆智命]]<ref name="cho">{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/higashiyamato/5762/|title=豊鹿嶋神社|publisher=[[神社庁|東京都神社庁]]|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>。本殿は[[室町時代]]の[[1466年]]（[[文正]]元年）建立であり、現存する東京都最古の[[神社建築]]である<ref name="cho"/>。
== 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[707年]]（[[慶雲]]4年）、[[武蔵国]]に来た[[鬼|鬼神]]を常陸峯に鎮めて、[[天智天皇]]の第四の姫宮と[[蘇我倉山田石川麻呂|蘇我山田石川麿]]が建立したことに始まる<ref name="book">{{Cite book |和書 |author=[[植田孟縉]]|year=1993|title=武蔵名勝図会 |page= |publisher=慶友社|location= |isbn=4874490808}}</ref>。以来、芋窪地域の鎮守として住民により守られてきた<ref name="cho"/>。
[[江戸時代]]には13石の[[社領]]を与えられていた<ref name="city">{{Cite web|title=11 豊鹿島神社|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,5862,360,622,html|publisher=東大和市|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>。
[[1993年]]（[[平成]]5年）に行われた[[拝殿]]の大改修において、『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿}}に記載されていた通り、[[文正]]元年[[10月3日]]の[[棟札]]が発見され、創建年が実証された。また、この改修で計5枚の棟札が発見され、改修前の拝殿は[[1858年]]（[[安政]]5年）に完成したことが確認された<ref name="book"/>。
== 文化財 == ; 東京都指定有形文化財（建造物） * 豊鹿島神社本殿 1棟 附・棟札 1枚 - 1964年（昭和39年）11月21日に都重宝として指定、1976年（昭和51年）7月1日に種別変更<ref name="to">{{Cite web|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=205|title=豊鹿島神社本殿|website=東京都文化財情報データベース|publisher=[[東京都教育委員会|東京都教育庁]]地域教育支援部|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : [[流造|一間社流造]]の本殿で[[こけら葺]]箱棟付、桁行（間口）は170.7cm、身舎の梁間（奥行）は152.4cm、向拝の梁間は140.4cmである<ref name="to"/>。現状は外気や風雨から保護するための[[覆堂|覆殿]]の中にあり、外から観察することはできない<ref name="cho"/>。建築年代について、かつては本殿内に保管されていた棟札にあった年紀通りに1550年（[[天文 (元号)|天文]]19年）とされてきたが、1992年（平成4年）から2年に及ぶ解体修理の際、大工二郎三郎近吉が1466年（文正元年）に建立した旨を記した棟札が新たに発見され、また、古くは厚板葺の屋根であったことも分かった<ref name="to"/>。
; 東大和市指定文化財（市重宝） * 豊鹿島神社本殿の木製[[狛犬]] - 1983年（昭和58年）3月1日指定<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|title=東大和市の文化財|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,2279,359,html|publisher=東大和市|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : 木製、本殿と同時期に作られた可能性が高いとされる<ref name="bunka"/>。 * 豊鹿島神社の[[獅子頭]] - 1983年（昭和58年）3月1日指定<ref name="bunka"/> : 江戸時代後期の作か、かつて奉納されていた[[獅子舞]]に用いられたとみられる<ref name="bunka"/>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=巻ノ120多磨郡ノ32|chapter=芋久保村|id={{NDLJP|763994/78}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[東京都の神社一覧]] * [[東京都指定文化財一覧]]

[[江戸時代]]には13石の[[社領]]を与えられていた<ref name="city">{{Cite web|title=11 豊鹿島神社|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,5862,360,622,html|publisher=東大和市|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>。
[[1993年]]（[[平成]]5年）に行われた[[拝殿]]の大改修において、『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に記載されていた通り{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 芋久保村 鹿島神社}}、[[文正]]元年[[10月3日]]の[[棟札]]が発見され、創建年が実証された。また、この改修で計5枚の棟札が発見され、改修前の拝殿は[[1858年]]（[[安政]]5年）に完成したことが確認された<ref name="book"/>。
== 文化財 == ; 東京都指定有形文化財（建造物） * 豊鹿島神社本殿 1棟 附・棟札 1枚 - 1964年（昭和39年）11月21日に都重宝として指定、1976年（昭和51年）7月1日に種別変更<ref name="to">{{Cite web|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=205|title=豊鹿島神社本殿|website=東京都文化財情報データベース|publisher=[[東京都教育委員会|東京都教育庁]]地域教育支援部|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : [[流造|一間社流造]]の本殿で[[こけら葺]]箱棟付、桁行（間口）は170.7cm、身舎の梁間（奥行）は152.4cm、向拝の梁間は140.4cmである<ref name="to"/>。現状は外気や風雨から保護するための[[覆堂|覆殿]]の中にあり、外から観察することはできない<ref name="cho"/>。建築年代について、かつては本殿内に保管されていた棟札にあった年紀通りに1550年（[[天文 (元号)|天文]]19年）とされてきたが、1992年（平成4年）から2年に及ぶ解体修理の際、大工二郎三郎近吉が1466年（文正元年）に建立した旨を記した棟札が新たに発見され、また、古くは厚板葺の屋根であったことも分かった<ref name="to"/>。
; 東大和市指定文化財（市重宝） * 豊鹿島神社本殿の木製[[狛犬]] - 1983年（昭和58年）3月1日指定<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|title=東大和市の文化財|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,2279,359,html|publisher=東大和市|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : 木製、本殿と同時期に作られた可能性が高いとされる<ref name="bunka"/>。 * 豊鹿島神社の[[獅子頭]] - 1983年（昭和58年）3月1日指定<ref name="bunka"/> : 江戸時代後期の作か、かつて奉納されていた[[獅子舞]]に用いられたとみられる<ref name="bunka"/>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=巻ノ120多磨郡ノ32|chapter=芋久保村 鹿島神社|id={{NDLJP|763994/79}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 芋久保村 鹿島神社}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[東京都の神社一覧]] * [[東京都指定文化財一覧]]

revision.text:
{{表記揺れ案内|表記1=豊鹿嶋神社|表記2=豊鹿島神社|表記3=|議論ページ=}} {{神社 |名称=豊鹿嶋神社 |画像=[[File:豊鹿嶋神社正面より.jpg|300px]] |所在地=[[東京都]][[東大和市]]芋窪1-2067<ref name="cho"/> |位置=<!--{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|45|28.5|N|139|24|46.2|E|scale:30000|display=title}}--> |祭神=武御加豆智命 |社格=[[郷社]] |創建=1466年 |例祭= |地図=Japan Tokyo|緯度度=35|緯度分=45|緯度秒=28.5|経度度=139|経度分=24|経度秒=46.2}} '''豊鹿嶋神社'''（とよかしまじんじゃ）とは、[[東京都]][[東大和市]]芋窪にある[[神社]]。祭神は[[タケミカヅチ|武御加豆智命]]<ref name="cho">{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/higashiyamato/5762/|title=豊鹿嶋神社|publisher=[[神社庁|東京都神社庁]]|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>。本殿は[[室町時代]]の[[1466年]]（[[文正]]元年）建立であり、現存する東京都最古の[[神社建築]]である<ref name="cho"/>。
== 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[707年]]（[[慶雲]]4年）、[[武蔵国]]に来た[[鬼|鬼神]]を常陸峯に鎮めて、[[天智天皇]]の第四の姫宮と[[蘇我倉山田石川麻呂|蘇我山田石川麿]]が建立したことに始まる<ref name="book">{{Cite book |和書 |author=植田孟縉|authorlink=植田孟縉|year=1993|title=武蔵名勝図会 |page= |publisher=慶友社|location= |isbn=4874490808}}</ref>。以来、芋窪地域の鎮守として住民により守られてきた<ref name="cho"/>。
[[江戸時代]]には13石の[[社領]]を与えられていた<ref name="city">{{Cite web|title=11 豊鹿島神社|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,5862,360,622,html|publisher=東大和市|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>。
[[1993年]]（[[平成]]5年）に行われた[[拝殿]]の大改修において、『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に記載されていた通り{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 芋久保村 鹿島神社}}、[[文正]]元年[[10月3日]]の[[棟札]]が発見され、創建年が実証された。また、この改修で計5枚の棟札が発見され、改修前の拝殿は[[1858年]]（[[安政]]5年）に完成したことが確認された<ref name="book"/>。
== 文化財 == ; 東京都指定有形文化財（建造物） * 豊鹿島神社本殿 1棟 附・棟札 1枚 - 1964年（昭和39年）11月21日に都重宝として指定、1976年（昭和51年）7月1日に種別変更<ref name="to">{{Cite web|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=205|title=豊鹿島神社本殿|website=東京都文化財情報データベース|publisher=[[東京都教育委員会|東京都教育庁]]地域教育支援部|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : [[流造|一間社流造]]の本殿で[[こけら葺]]箱棟付、桁行（間口）は170.7cm、身舎の梁間（奥行）は152.4cm、向拝の梁間は140.4cmである<ref name="to"/>。現状は外気や風雨から保護するための[[覆堂|覆殿]]の中にあり、外から観察することはできない<ref name="cho"/>。建築年代について、かつては本殿内に保管されていた棟札にあった年紀通りに1550年（[[天文 (元号)|天文]]19年）とされてきたが、1992年（平成4年）から2年に及ぶ解体修理の際、大工二郎三郎近吉が1466年（文正元年）に建立した旨を記した棟札が新たに発見され、また、古くは厚板葺の屋根であったことも分かった<ref name="to"/>。
; 東大和市指定文化財（市重宝） * 豊鹿島神社本殿の木製[[狛犬]] - 1983年（昭和58年）3月1日指定<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|title=東大和市の文化財|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,2279,359,html|publisher=東大和市|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : 木製、本殿と同時期に作られた可能性が高いとされる<ref name="bunka"/>。 * 豊鹿島神社の[[獅子頭]] - 1983年（昭和58年）3月1日指定<ref name="bunka"/> : 江戸時代後期の作か、かつて奉納されていた[[獅子舞]]に用いられたとみられる<ref name="bunka"/>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=巻ノ120多磨郡ノ32|chapter=芋久保村 鹿島神社|id={{NDLJP|763994/79}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 芋久保村 鹿島神社}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[東京都の神社一覧]] * [[東京都指定文化財一覧]]

revision.text:
{{表記揺れ案内|表記1=豊鹿嶋神社|表記2=豊鹿島神社|表記3=|議論ページ=}} {{神社 |名称=豊鹿嶋神社 |画像=[[File:豊鹿嶋神社正面より.jpg|300px]] |所在地=[[東京都]][[東大和市]]芋窪1-2067<ref name="cho"/> |位置=<!--{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|45|28.5|N|139|24|46.2|E|scale:30000|display=title}}--> |祭神=武御加豆智命 |社格=[[郷社]] |創建=1466年 |例祭= |地図=Japan Tokyo|緯度度=35|緯度分=45|緯度秒=28.5|経度度=139|経度分=24|経度秒=46.2}} '''豊鹿嶋神社'''（とよかしまじんじゃ）とは、[[東京都]][[東大和市]]芋窪にある[[神社]]。祭神は[[タケミカヅチ|武御加豆智命]]<ref name="cho">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/higashiyamato/5762/|title=豊鹿嶋神社|publisher=[[神社庁|東京都神社庁]]|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>。本殿は[[室町時代]]の[[1466年]]（[[文正]]元年）建立であり、現存する東京都最古の[[神社建築]]である<ref name="cho"/>。
== 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[707年]]（[[慶雲]]4年）、[[武蔵国]]に来た[[鬼|鬼神]]を常陸峯に鎮めて、[[天智天皇]]の第四の姫宮と[[蘇我倉山田石川麻呂|蘇我山田石川麿]]が建立したことに始まる<ref name="book">{{Cite book |和書 |author=植田孟縉|authorlink=植田孟縉|year=1993|title=武蔵名勝図会 |page= |publisher=慶友社|location= |isbn=4874490808}}</ref>。以来、芋窪地域の鎮守として住民により守られてきた<ref name="cho"/>。
[[江戸時代]]には13石の[[社領]]を与えられていた<ref name="city">{{Cite web|和書|title=11 豊鹿島神社|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,5862,360,622,html|publisher=東大和市|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>。
[[1993年]]（[[平成]]5年）に行われた[[拝殿]]の大改修において、『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に記載されていた通り{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 芋久保村 鹿島神社}}、[[文正]]元年[[10月3日]]の[[棟札]]が発見され、創建年が実証された。また、この改修で計5枚の棟札が発見され、改修前の拝殿は[[1858年]]（[[安政]]5年）に完成したことが確認された<ref name="book"/>。
== 文化財 == ; 東京都指定有形文化財（建造物） * 豊鹿島神社本殿 1棟 附・棟札 1枚 - 1964年（昭和39年）11月21日に都重宝として指定、1976年（昭和51年）7月1日に種別変更<ref name="to">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=205|title=豊鹿島神社本殿|website=東京都文化財情報データベース|publisher=[[東京都教育委員会|東京都教育庁]]地域教育支援部|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : [[流造|一間社流造]]の本殿で[[こけら葺]]箱棟付、桁行（間口）は170.7cm、身舎の梁間（奥行）は152.4cm、向拝の梁間は140.4cmである<ref name="to"/>。現状は外気や風雨から保護するための[[覆堂|覆殿]]の中にあり、外から観察することはできない<ref name="cho"/>。建築年代について、かつては本殿内に保管されていた棟札にあった年紀通りに1550年（[[天文 (元号)|天文]]19年）とされてきたが、1992年（平成4年）から2年に及ぶ解体修理の際、大工二郎三郎近吉が1466年（文正元年）に建立した旨を記した棟札が新たに発見され、また、古くは厚板葺の屋根であったことも分かった<ref name="to"/>。
; 東大和市指定文化財（市重宝） * 豊鹿島神社本殿の木製[[狛犬]] - 1983年（昭和58年）3月1日指定<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|和書|title=東大和市の文化財|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,2279,359,html|publisher=東大和市|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : 木製、本殿と同時期に作られた可能性が高いとされる<ref name="bunka"/>。 * 豊鹿島神社の[[獅子頭]] - 1983年（昭和58年）3月1日指定<ref name="bunka"/> : 江戸時代後期の作か、かつて奉納されていた[[獅子舞]]に用いられたとみられる<ref name="bunka"/>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=巻ノ120多磨郡ノ32|chapter=芋久保村 鹿島神社|id={{NDLJP|763994/79}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 芋久保村 鹿島神社}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[東京都の神社一覧]] * [[東京都指定文化財一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:とよかしましんしや}} [[Category:鹿島神社]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:東京都指定有形文化財]] [[Category:東京都内の区市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:東大和市の建築物]] [[Category:東大和市の歴史]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attriubtion, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too.]][[ja:豊鹿嶋神社]][[Category:qq]]

{{表記揺れ案内|表記1=豊鹿嶋神社|表記2=豊鹿島神社|表記3=|議論ページ=}} {{神社 |名称=豊鹿嶋神社 |画像=[[File:豊鹿嶋神社正面より.jpg|300px]] |所在地=[[東京都]][[東大和市]]芋窪1-2067<ref name="cho"/> |位置=<!--{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|45|28.5|N|139|24|46.2|E|scale:30000|display=title}}--> |祭神=武御加豆智命 |社格=[[郷社]] |創建=1466年 |例祭= |地図=Japan Tokyo|緯度度=35|緯度分=45|緯度秒=28.5|経度度=139|経度分=24|経度秒=46.2}} '''豊鹿嶋神社'''（とよかしまじんじゃ）とは、[[東京都]][[東大和市]]芋窪にある[[神社]]。祭神は[[タケミカヅチ|武御加豆智命]]<ref name="cho">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/higashiyamato/5762/|title=豊鹿嶋神社|publisher=[[神社庁|東京都神社庁]]|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>。本殿は[[室町時代]]の[[1466年]]（[[文正]]元年）建立であり、現存する東京都最古の[[神社建築]]である<ref name="cho"/>。
== 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[707年]]（[[慶雲]]4年）、[[武蔵国]]に来た[[鬼|鬼神]]を常陸峯に鎮めて、[[天智天皇]]の第四の姫宮と[[蘇我倉山田石川麻呂|蘇我山田石川麿]]が建立したことに始まる<ref name="book">{{Cite book |和書 |author=植田孟縉|authorlink=植田孟縉|year=1993|title=武蔵名勝図会 |page= |publisher=慶友社|location= |isbn=4874490808}}</ref>。以来、芋窪地域の鎮守として住民により守られてきた<ref name="cho"/>。
[[江戸時代]]には13石の[[社領]]を与えられていた<ref name="city">{{Cite web|和書|title=11 豊鹿島神社|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,5862,360,622,html|publisher=東大和市|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>。
[[1993年]]（[[平成]]5年）に行われた[[拝殿]]の大改修において、『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に記載されていた通り{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 芋久保村 鹿島神社}}、[[文正]]元年[[10月3日]]の[[棟札]]が発見され、創建年が実証された。また、この改修で計5枚の棟札が発見され、改修前の拝殿は[[1858年]]（[[安政]]5年）に完成したことが確認された<ref name="book"/>。
== 文化財 == ; 東京都指定有形文化財（建造物） * 豊鹿島神社本殿 1棟 附・棟札 1枚 - 1964年（昭和39年）11月21日に都重宝として指定、1976年（昭和51年）7月1日に種別変更<ref name="to">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=205|title=豊鹿島神社本殿|website=東京都文化財情報データベース|publisher=[[東京都教育委員会|東京都教育庁]]地域教育支援部|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : [[流造|一間社流造]]の本殿で[[こけら葺]]箱棟付、桁行（間口）は170.7cm、身舎の梁間（奥行）は152.4cm、向拝の梁間は140.4cmである<ref name="to"/>。現状は外気や風雨から保護するための[[覆堂|覆殿]]の中にあり、外から観察することはできない<ref name="cho"/>。建築年代について、かつては本殿内に保管されていた棟札にあった年紀通りに1550年（[[天文 (元号)|天文]]19年）とされてきたが、1992年（平成4年）から2年に及ぶ解体修理の際、大工二郎三郎近吉が1466年（文正元年）に建立した旨を記した棟札が新たに発見され、また、古くは厚板葺の屋根であったことも分かった<ref name="to"/>。
; 東大和市指定文化財（市重宝） * 豊鹿島神社本殿の木製[[狛犬]] - 1983年（昭和58年）3月1日指定<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|和書|title=東大和市の文化財|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,2279,359,html|publisher=東大和市|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : 木製、本殿と同時期に作られた可能性が高いとされる<ref name="bunka"/>。 * 豊鹿島神社の[[獅子頭]] - 1983年（昭和58年）3月1日指定<ref name="bunka"/> : 江戸時代後期の作か、かつて奉納されていた[[獅子舞]]に用いられたとみられる<ref name="bunka"/>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=巻ノ120多磨郡ノ32|chapter=芋久保村 鹿島神社|id={{NDLJP|763994/79}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 芋久保村 鹿島神社}}}}

{{表記揺れ案内|表記1=豊鹿嶋神社|表記2=豊鹿島神社|表記3=|議論ページ=}} {{Shrine | Name = Toyokashima Shrine | Image = [[File:豊鹿嶋神社正面より.jpg|300px]] | Location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Higashiyamato, Tokyo|ja|東大和市}} Imokubo 1-2067<ref name="cho"/> | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|45|28.5|N|139|24|46.2|E|type:landmark_region:JP-13}} | Deity = {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御加豆智命}} | Status = {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} | Established = 1466 | Festival = | Map = Japan Tokyo }}
'''Toyokashima Shrine''' (豊鹿嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyamato, Tokyo|ja|東大和市}}, Tokyo. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御加豆智命}}<ref name="cho">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/higashiyamato/5762/|title=豊鹿嶋神社|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|東京都神社庁}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>. The shrine's **main hall (honden)**, built in 1466 during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, is the oldest existing {{ill|Shinto shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} in Tokyo<ref name="cho"/>.
== History ==
According to the shrine tradition, in **707 CE** ({{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 4), a **demon god** that arrived in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}} was pacified at **Hitachi Peak**, and the shrine was founded by the **fourth princess of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}** and {{ill|Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|ja|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂}}<ref name="book">{{Cite book |ja|author={{ill|Ueda Mōshin|ja|植田孟縉}}|year=1993|title=武蔵名勝図会 |page= |publisher=慶友社|isbn=4874490808}}</ref>. Since then, it has been protected by the residents of the **Imokubo area** as their guardian shrine<ref name="cho"/>.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was granted **13 koku** of shrine land ({{ill|Sharyō|ja|社領}})<ref name="city">{{Cite web|ja|title=11 豊鹿島神社|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,5862,360,622,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>.
In **1993** ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}} 5), during a major **restoration of the haiden** (worship hall), a **tōsatsu (ridge plaque)** dated to **1466** ({{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正}} 1) was discovered, confirming the shrine's founding year, as previously recorded in the **Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō** ({{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}). Additionally, five other **tōsatsu** were found, revealing that the haiden before the restoration was constructed in **1858** ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 5)<ref name="book"/>.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Tokyo Designated Tangible Cultural Property (Architecture) ===
* **Toyokashima Shrine Honden (Main Hall), 1 building** - designated as a Tokyo Important Cultural Property on **November 21, 1964** (Showa 39), reclassified on **July 1, 1976**<ref name="to">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=205|title=豊鹿島神社本殿|website=Tokyo Cultural Property Database|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|ja|東京都教育委員会}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : The shrine features a **single-bay nagare-zukuri** ({{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}) main hall with **kokera-buki (wooden shingles)** and a **box ridge**. The dimensions are **170.7 cm in width**, with the **shrine core (moya)** having a **depth of 152.4 cm**, and the **front eaves (kōhai)** extending **140.4 cm**<ref name="to"/>. The structure is currently housed within a **protective covering hall** ({{ill|Oudou|ja|覆堂}}) to shield it from the elements, making it inaccessible for direct viewing<ref name="cho"/>. Previously, the **tōsatsu** stored inside the main hall was believed to indicate the construction year as **1550** ({{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 19), but a **new tōsatsu** discovered during the **1992–1994 dismantling repairs** confirmed the construction date as **1466** (Bunshō 1), with records of a **master carpenter named Jirōsaburō Chikayoshi**. The roof was also originally made of **thick wooden planks (atsuita-buki)**<ref name="to"/>.
=== Higashiyamato City Designated Cultural Property (Municipal Treasure) ===
* **Toyokashima Shrine Wooden Komainu (Guardian Lion-Dogs)** - designated on **March 1, 1983** (Showa 58)<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|ja|title=東大和市の文化財|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,2279,359,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : These wooden komainu are believed to have been crafted **during the same period as the honden**<ref name="bunka"/>.
* **Toyokashima Shrine Shishigashira (Lion Head)** - designated on **March 1, 1983**<ref name="bunka"/> : This lion head, likely from the **late Edo period**, was used in **lion dances** ({{ill|Shishimai|ja|獅子舞}}) that were formerly performed at the shrine<ref name="bunka"/>.

{{表記揺れ案内|表記1=豊鹿嶋神社|表記2=豊鹿島神社|表記3=|議論ページ=}} {{Shrine | Name = Toyokashima Shrine | Image = [[File:豊鹿嶋神社正面より.jpg|300px]] | Location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Higashiyamato, Tokyo|ja|東大和市}} Imokubo 1-2067<ref name="cho"/> | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|45|28.5|N|139|24|46.2|E|type:landmark_region:JP-13}} | Deity = {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御加豆智命}} | Status = {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} | Established = 1466 | Festival = | Map = Japan Tokyo }}
'''Toyokashima Shrine''' (豊鹿嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyamato, Tokyo|ja|東大和市}}, Tokyo. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御加豆智命}}<ref name="cho">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/higashiyamato/5762/|title=豊鹿嶋神社|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|東京都神社庁}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>. The shrine's **main hall (honden)**, built in 1466 during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, is the oldest existing {{ill|Shinto shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} in Tokyo<ref name="cho"/>.
== History ==
According to the shrine tradition, in **707 CE** ({{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 4), a **demon god** that arrived in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}} was pacified at **Hitachi Peak**, and the shrine was founded by the **fourth princess of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}** and {{ill|Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|ja|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂}}<ref name="book">{{Cite book |ja|author={{ill|Ueda Mōshin|ja|植田孟縉}}|year=1993|title=武蔵名勝図会 |page= |publisher=慶友社|isbn=4874490808}}</ref>. Since then, it has been protected by the residents of the **Imokubo area** as their guardian shrine<ref name="cho"/>.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was granted **13 koku** of shrine land ({{ill|Sharyō|ja|社領}})<ref name="city">{{Cite web|ja|title=11 豊鹿島神社|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,5862,360,622,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>.

Additionally, five other **tōsatsu** were found, revealing that the haiden before the restoration was constructed in **1858** ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 5)<ref name="book"/>.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Tokyo Designated Tangible Cultural Property (Architecture) ===
* **Toyokashima Shrine Honden (Main Hall), 1 building** - designated as a Tokyo Important Cultural Property on **November 21, 1964** (Showa 39), reclassified on **July 1, 1976**<ref name="to">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=205|title=豊鹿島神社本殿|website=Tokyo Cultural Property Database|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|ja|東京都教育委員会}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : The shrine features a **single-bay nagare-zukuri** ({{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}) main hall with **kokera-buki (wooden shingles)** and a **box ridge**. The dimensions are **170.7 cm in width**, with the **shrine core (moya)** having a **depth of 152.4 cm**, and the **front eaves (kōhai)** extending **140.4 cm**<ref name="to"/>. The structure is currently housed within a **protective covering hall** ({{ill|Oudou|ja|覆堂}}) to shield it from the elements, making it inaccessible for direct viewing<ref name="cho"/>. Previously, the **tōsatsu** stored inside the main hall was believed to indicate the construction year as **1550** ({{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 19), but a **new tōsatsu** discovered during the **1992–1994 dismantling repairs** confirmed the construction date as **1466** (Bunshō 1), with records of a **master carpenter named Jirōsaburō Chikayoshi**. The roof was also originally made of **thick wooden planks (atsuita-buki)**<ref name="to"/>.
=== Higashiyamato City Designated Cultural Property (Municipal Treasure) ===
* **Toyokashima Shrine Wooden Komainu (Guardian Lion-Dogs)** - designated on **March 1, 1983** (Showa 58)<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|ja|title=東大和市の文化財|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,2279,359,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : These wooden komainu are believed to have been crafted **during the same period as the honden**<ref name="bunka"/>.
* **Toyokashima Shrine Shishigashira (Lion Head)** - designated on **March 1, 1983**<ref name="bunka"/> : This lion head, likely from the **late Edo period**, was used in **lion dances** ({{ill|Shishimai|ja|獅子舞}}) that were formerly performed at the shrine<ref name="bunka"/>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=Volume 120, Tama District No. 32|chapter=Imokubo Village Kashima Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763994/79}}|publisher={{ill|Imperial Ministry of Home Affairs|ja|内務省地理局}}|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō}}}}

{{表記揺れ案内|表記1=豊鹿嶋神社|表記2=豊鹿島神社|表記3=|議論ページ=}} {{Shrine | Name = Toyokashima Shrine | Image = [[File:豊鹿嶋神社正面より.jpg|300px]] | Location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Higashiyamato, Tokyo|ja|東大和市}} Imokubo 1-2067<ref name="cho"/> | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|45|28.5|N|139|24|46.2|E|type:landmark_region:JP-13}} | Deity = {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御加豆智命}} | Status = {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} | Established = 1466 | Festival = | Map = Japan Tokyo }}
'''Toyokashima Shrine''' (豊鹿嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyamato, Tokyo|ja|東大和市}}, Tokyo. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御加豆智命}}<ref name="cho">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/higashiyamato/5762/|title=豊鹿嶋神社|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|東京都神社庁}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>. The shrine's **main hall (honden)**, built in 1466 during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, is the oldest existing {{ill|Shinto shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} in Tokyo<ref name="cho"/>.
== History ==
According to the shrine tradition, in **707 CE** ({{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 4), a **demon god** that arrived in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}} was pacified at **Hitachi Peak**, and the shrine was founded by the **fourth princess of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}** and {{ill|Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|ja|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂}}<ref name="book">{{Cite book |ja|author={{ill|Ueda Mōshin|ja|植田孟縉}}|year=1993|title=武蔵名勝図会 |page= |publisher=慶友社|isbn=4874490808}}</ref>. Since then, it has been protected by the residents of the **Imokubo area** as their guardian shrine<ref name="cho"/>.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was granted **13 koku** of shrine land ({{ill|Sharyō|ja|社領}})<ref name="city">{{Cite web|ja|title=11 豊鹿島神社|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,5862,360,622,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>.
In **1993** ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}} 5), during a major **restoration of the haiden** (worship hall), a **tōsatsu (ridge plaque)** dated to **1466** ({{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正}} 1) was discovered, confirming the shrine's founding year, as previously recorded in the **Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō** ({{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}). Additionally, five other **tōsatsu** were found, revealing that the haiden before the restoration was constructed in **1858** ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 5)<ref name="book"/>.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Tokyo Designated Tangible Cultural Property (Architecture) ===
* **Toyokashima Shrine Honden (Main Hall), 1 building** - designated as a Tokyo Important Cultural Property on **November 21, 1964** (Showa 39), reclassified on **July 1, 1976**<ref name="to">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=205|title=豊鹿島神社本殿|website=Tokyo Cultural Property Database|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|ja|東京都教育委員会}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : The shrine features a **single-bay nagare-zukuri** ({{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}) main hall with **kokera-buki (wooden shingles)** and a **box ridge**. The dimensions are **170.7 cm in width**, with the **shrine core (moya)** having a **depth of 152.4 cm**, and the **front eaves (kōhai)** extending **140.4 cm**<ref name="to"/>. The structure is currently housed within a **protective covering hall** ({{ill|Oudou|ja|覆堂}}) to shield it from the elements, making it inaccessible for direct viewing<ref name="cho"/>. Previously, the **tōsatsu** stored inside the main hall was believed to indicate the construction year as **1550** ({{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 19), but a **new tōsatsu** discovered during the **1992–1994 dismantling repairs** confirmed the construction date as **1466** (Bunshō 1), with records of a **master carpenter named Jirōsaburō Chikayoshi**. The roof was also originally made of **thick wooden planks (atsuita-buki)**<ref name="to"/>.
=== Higashiyamato City Designated Cultural Property (Municipal Treasure) ===
* **Toyokashima Shrine Wooden Komainu (Guardian Lion-Dogs)** - designated on **March 1, 1983** (Showa 58)<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|ja|title=東大和市の文化財|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,2279,359,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : These wooden komainu are believed to have been crafted **during the same period as the honden**<ref name="bunka"/>.
* **Toyokashima Shrine Shishigashira (Lion Head)** - designated on **March 1, 1983**<ref name="bunka"/> : This lion head, likely from the **late Edo period**, was used in **lion dances** ({{ill|Shishimai|ja|獅子舞}}) that were formerly performed at the shrine<ref name="bunka"/>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=Volume 120, Tama District No. 32|chapter=Imokubo Village Kashima Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763994/79}}|publisher={{ill|Imperial Ministry of Home Affairs|ja|内務省地理局}}|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō}}}}

{{表記揺れ案内|表記1=豊鹿嶋神社|表記2=豊鹿島神社|表記3=|議論ページ=}} {{Shrine | Name = Toyokashima Shrine | Image = [[File:豊鹿嶋神社正面より.jpg|300px]] | Location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Higashiyamato, Tokyo|ja|東大和市}} Imokubo 1-2067<ref name="cho"/> | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|45|28.5|N|139|24|46.2|E|type:landmark_region:JP-13}} | Deity = {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御加豆智命}} | Status = {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} | Established = 1466 | Festival = | Map = Japan Tokyo }}
'''Toyokashima Shrine''' (豊鹿嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyamato, Tokyo|ja|東大和市}}, Tokyo. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御加豆智命}}<ref name="cho">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/higashiyamato/5762/|title=豊鹿嶋神社|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|東京都神社庁}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>. The shrine's **main hall (honden)**, built in 1466 during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, is the oldest existing {{ill|Shinto shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} in Tokyo<ref name="cho"/>.
== History ==
According to the shrine tradition, in **707 CE** ({{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 4), a **demon god** that arrived in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}} was pacified at **Hitachi Peak**, and the shrine was founded by the **fourth princess of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}** and {{ill|Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|ja|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂}}<ref name="book">{{Cite book |ja|author={{ill|Ueda Mōshin|ja|植田孟縉}}|year=1993|title=武蔵名勝図会 |page= |publisher=慶友社|isbn=4874490808}}</ref>. Since then, it has been protected by the residents of the **Imokubo area** as their guardian shrine<ref name="cho"/>.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was granted **13 koku** of shrine land ({{ill|Sharyō|ja|社領}})<ref name="city">{{Cite web|ja|title=11 豊鹿島神社|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,5862,360,622,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>.
In **1993** ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}} 5), during a major **restoration of the haiden** (worship hall), a **tōsatsu (ridge plaque)** dated to **1466** ({{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正}} 1) was discovered, confirming the shrine's founding year, as previously recorded in the **Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō** ({{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}). Additionally, five other **tōsatsu** were found, revealing that the haiden before the restoration was constructed in **1858** ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 5)<ref name="book"/>.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Tokyo Designated Tangible Cultural Property (Architecture) ===
* **Toyokashima Shrine Honden (Main Hall), 1 building** - designated as a Tokyo Important Cultural Property on **November 21, 1964** (Showa 39), reclassified on **July 1, 1976**<ref name="to">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=205|title=豊鹿島神社本殿|website=Tokyo Cultural Property Database|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|ja|東京都教育委員会}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : The shrine features a **single-bay nagare-zukuri** ({{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}) main hall with **kokera-buki (wooden shingles)** and a **box ridge**. The dimensions are **170.7 cm in width**, with the **shrine core (moya)** having a **depth of 152.4 cm**, and the **front eaves (kōhai)** extending **140.4 cm**<ref name="to"/>. The structure is currently housed within a **protective covering hall** ({{ill|Oudou|ja|覆堂}}) to shield it from the elements, making it inaccessible for direct viewing<ref name="cho"/>. Previously, the **tōsatsu** stored inside the main hall was believed to indicate the construction year as **1550** ({{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 19), but a **new tōsatsu** discovered during the **1992–1994 dismantling repairs** confirmed the construction date as **1466** (Bunshō 1), with records of a **master carpenter named Jirōsaburō Chikayoshi**. The roof was also originally made of **thick wooden planks (atsuita-buki)**<ref name="to"/>.
=== Higashiyamato City Designated Cultural Property (Municipal Treasure) ===
* **Toyokashima Shrine Wooden Komainu (Guardian Lion-Dogs)** - designated on **March 1, 1983** (Showa 58)<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|ja|title=東大和市の文化財|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,2279,359,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : These wooden komainu are believed to have been crafted **during the same period as the honden**<ref name="bunka"/>.
* **Toyokashima Shrine Shishigashira (Lion Head)** - designated on **March 1, 1983**<ref name="bunka"/> : This lion head, likely from the **late Edo period**, was used in **lion dances** ({{ill|Shishimai|ja|獅子舞}}) that were formerly performed at the shrine<ref name="bunka"/>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=Volume 120, Tama District No. 32|chapter=Imokubo Village Kashima Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763994/79}}|publisher={{ill|Imperial Ministry of Home Affairs|ja|内務省地理局}}|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō}}}}

During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was granted '''13 koku''' of shrine land ({{ill|Sharyō|ja|社領}})<ref name="city">{{Cite web|ja|title=11 豊鹿島神社|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,5862,360,622,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>.
In '''1993''' ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}} 5), during a major '''restoration of the haiden''' (worship hall), a '''tōsatsu (ridge plaque)''' dated to '''1466''' ({{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正}} 1) was discovered, confirming the shrine's founding year, as previously recorded in the '''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō''' ({{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}). Additionally, five other '''tōsatsu''' were found, revealing that the haiden before the restoration was constructed in '''1858''' ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 5)<ref name="book"/>.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Tokyo Designated Tangible Cultural Property (Architecture) ===
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Honden (Main Hall), 1 building''' - designated as a Tokyo Important Cultural Property on '''November 21, 1964''' (Showa 39), reclassified on '''July 1, 1976'''<ref name="to">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=205|title=豊鹿島神社本殿|website=Tokyo Cultural Property Database|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|ja|東京都教育委員会}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : The shrine features a '''single-bay nagare-zukuri''' ({{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}) main hall with '''kokera-buki (wooden shingles)''' and a '''box ridge'''. The dimensions are '''170.7 cm in width''', with the '''shrine core (moya)''' having a '''depth of 152.4 cm''', and the '''front eaves (kōhai)''' extending '''140.4 cm'''<ref name="to"/>. The structure is currently housed within a '''protective covering hall''' ({{ill|Oudou|ja|覆堂}}) to shield it from the elements, making it inaccessible for direct viewing<ref name="cho"/>. Previously, the '''tōsatsu''' stored inside the main hall was believed to indicate the construction year as '''1550''' ({{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 19), but a '''new tōsatsu''' discovered during the '''1992–1994 dismantling repairs''' confirmed the construction date as '''1466''' (Bunshō 1), with records of a '''master carpenter named Jirōsaburō Chikayoshi'''. The roof was also originally made of '''thick wooden planks (atsuita-buki)'''<ref name="to"/>.
=== Higashiyamato City Designated Cultural Property (Municipal Treasure) ===
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Wooden Komainu (Guardian Lion-Dogs)''' - designated on '''March 1, 1983''' (Showa 58)<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|ja|title=東大和市の文化財|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,2279,359,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : These wooden komainu are believed to have been crafted '''during the same period as the honden'''<ref name="bunka"/>.
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Shishigashira (Lion Head)''' - designated on '''March 1, 1983'''<ref name="bunka"/> : This lion head, likely from the '''late Edo period''', was used in '''lion dances''' ({{ill|Shishimai|ja|獅子舞}}) that were formerly performed at the shrine<ref name="bunka"/>.

'''Toyokashima Shrine''' (豊鹿嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyamato, Tokyo|ja|東大和市}}, Tokyo. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御加豆智命}}<ref name="cho">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/higashiyamato/5762/|title=豊鹿嶋神社|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|東京都神社庁}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>. The shrine's '''main hall (honden)''', built in 1466 during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, is the oldest existing {{ill|Shinto shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} in Tokyo<ref name="cho"/>.
== History ==
According to the shrine tradition, in '''707 CE''' ({{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 4), a '''demon god''' that arrived in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}} was pacified at '''Hitachi Peak''', and the shrine was founded by the '''fourth princess of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}''' and {{ill|Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|ja|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂}}<ref name="book">{{Cite book |ja|author={{ill|Ueda Mōshin|ja|植田孟縉}}|year=1993|title=武蔵名勝図会 |page= |publisher=慶友社|isbn=4874490808}}</ref>. Since then, it has been protected by the residents of the '''Imokubo area''' as their guardian shrine<ref name="cho"/>.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was granted '''13 koku''' of shrine land ({{ill|Sharyō|ja|社領}})<ref name="city">{{Cite web|ja|title=11 豊鹿島神社|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,5862,360,622,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>.
In '''1993''' ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}} 5), during a major '''restoration of the haiden''' (worship hall), a '''tōsatsu (ridge plaque)''' dated to '''1466''' ({{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正}} 1) was discovered, confirming the shrine's founding year, as previously recorded in the '''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō''' ({{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}). Additionally, five other '''tōsatsu''' were found, revealing that the haiden before the restoration was constructed in '''1858''' ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 5)<ref name="book"/>.

The structure is currently housed within a '''protective covering hall''' ({{ill|Oudou|ja|覆堂}}) to shield it from the elements, making it inaccessible for direct viewing<ref name="cho"/>. The roof was also originally made of '''thick wooden planks (atsuita-buki)'''<ref name="to"/>.

* '''Toyokashima Shrine Wooden Komainu (Guardian Lion-Dogs)''' - designated on '''March 1, 1983''' (Showa 58)<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|ja|title=東大和市の文化財|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,2279,359,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : These wooden komainu are believed to have been crafted '''during the same period as the honden'''<ref name="bunka"/>.
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Shishigashira (Lion Head)''' - designated on '''March 1, 1983'''<ref name="bunka"/> : This lion head, likely from the '''late Edo period''', was used in '''lion dances''' ({{ill|Shishimai|ja|獅子舞}}) that were formerly performed at the shrine<ref name="bunka"/>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=Volume 120, Tama District No. 32|chapter=Imokubo Village Kashima Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763994/79}}|publisher={{ill|Imperial Ministry of Home Affairs|ja|内務省地理局}}|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō}}}}

revision.text:
{{表記揺れ案内|表記1=豊鹿嶋神社|表記2=豊鹿島神社|表記3=|議論ページ=}} {{Shrine | Name = Toyokashima Shrine | image = [[File:豊鹿嶋神社正面より.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Higashiyamato, Tokyo|ja|東大和市}} Imokubo 1-2067<ref name="cho"/> | coordinates = {{Coord|35|45|28.5|N|139|24|46.2|E|type:landmark_region:JP-13}} | deity = {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御加豆智命}} | Status = {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} | Established = 1466 | Festival = | Map = Japan Tokyo }}
'''Toyokashima Shrine''' (豊鹿嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyamato, Tokyo|ja|東大和市}}, Tokyo. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御加豆智命}}<ref name="cho">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/higashiyamato/5762/|title=豊鹿嶋神社|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|東京都神社庁}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>. The shrine's '''main hall (honden)''', built in 1466 during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, is the oldest existing {{ill|Shinto shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} in Tokyo<ref name="cho"/>.
== History ==
According to the shrine tradition, in '''707 CE''' ({{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 4), a '''demon god''' that arrived in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}} was pacified at '''Hitachi Peak''', and the shrine was founded by the '''fourth princess of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}''' and {{ill|Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|ja|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂}}<ref name="book">{{Cite book |ja|author={{ill|Ueda Mōshin|ja|植田孟縉}}|year=1993|title=武蔵名勝図会 |page= |publisher=慶友社|isbn=4874490808}}</ref>. Since then, it has been protected by the residents of the '''Imokubo area''' as their guardian shrine<ref name="cho"/>.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was granted '''13 koku''' of shrine land ({{ill|Sharyō|ja|社領}})<ref name="city">{{Cite web|ja|title=11 豊鹿島神社|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,5862,360,622,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>.
In '''1993''' ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}} 5), during a major '''restoration of the haiden''' (worship hall), a '''tōsatsu (ridge plaque)''' dated to '''1466''' ({{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正}} 1) was discovered, confirming the shrine's founding year, as previously recorded in the '''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō''' ({{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}). Additionally, five other '''tōsatsu''' were found, revealing that the haiden before the restoration was constructed in '''1858''' ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 5)<ref name="book"/>.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Tokyo Designated Tangible Cultural Property (Architecture) ===
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Honden (Main Hall), 1 building''' - designated as a Tokyo Important Cultural Property on '''November 21, 1964''' (Showa 39), reclassified on '''July 1, 1976'''<ref name="to">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=205|title=豊鹿島神社本殿|website=Tokyo Cultural Property Database|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|ja|東京都教育委員会}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : The shrine features a '''single-bay nagare-zukuri''' ({{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}) main hall with '''kokera-buki (wooden shingles)''' and a '''box ridge'''. The dimensions are '''170.7 cm in width''', with the '''shrine core (moya)''' having a '''depth of 152.4 cm''', and the '''front eaves (kōhai)''' extending '''140.4 cm'''<ref name="to"/>. The structure is currently housed within a '''protective covering hall''' ({{ill|Oudou|ja|覆堂}}) to shield it from the elements, making it inaccessible for direct viewing<ref name="cho"/>. Previously, the '''tōsatsu''' stored inside the main hall was believed to indicate the construction year as '''1550''' ({{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 19), but a '''new tōsatsu''' discovered during the '''1992–1994 dismantling repairs''' confirmed the construction date as '''1466''' (Bunshō 1), with records of a '''master carpenter named Jirōsaburō Chikayoshi'''. The roof was also originally made of '''thick wooden planks (atsuita-buki)'''<ref name="to"/>.
=== Higashiyamato City Designated Cultural Property (Municipal Treasure) ===
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Wooden Komainu (Guardian Lion-Dogs)''' - designated on '''March 1, 1983''' (Showa 58)<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|ja|title=東大和市の文化財|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,2279,359,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : These wooden komainu are believed to have been crafted '''during the same period as the honden'''<ref name="bunka"/>.
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Shishigashira (Lion Head)''' - designated on '''March 1, 1983'''<ref name="bunka"/> : This lion head, likely from the '''late Edo period''', was used in '''lion dances''' ({{ill|Shishimai|ja|獅子舞}}) that were formerly performed at the shrine<ref name="bunka"/>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=Volume 120, Tama District No. 32|chapter=Imokubo Village Kashima Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763994/79}}|publisher={{ill|Imperial Ministry of Home Affairs|ja|内務省地理局}}|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo|ja|東京都の神社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tokyo-designated cultural properties|ja|東京都指定文化財一覧}}

{{表記揺れ案内|表記1=豊鹿嶋神社|表記2=豊鹿島神社|表記3=|議論ページ=}} {{Shrine | Name = Toyokashima Shrine | image = [[File:豊鹿嶋神社正面より.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Higashiyamato, Tokyo|ja|東大和市}} Imokubo 1-2067<ref name="cho"/> | coordinates = {{Coord|35|45|28.5|N|139|24|46.2|E|type:landmark_region:JP-13}} | deity = {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御加豆智命}} | Status = {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} | Established = 1466 | Festival = | Map = Japan Tokyo }}
'''Toyokashima Shrine''' (豊鹿嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyamato, Tokyo|ja|東大和市}}, Tokyo. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御加豆智命}}<ref name="cho">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/higashiyamato/5762/|title=豊鹿嶋神社|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|東京都神社庁}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>. The shrine's '''main hall (honden)''', built in 1466 during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, is the oldest existing {{ill|Shinto shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} in Tokyo<ref name="cho"/>.
== History ==
According to the shrine tradition, in '''707 CE''' ({{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 4), a '''demon god''' that arrived in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}} was pacified at '''Hitachi Peak''', and the shrine was founded by the '''fourth princess of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}''' and {{ill|Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|ja|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂}}<ref name="book">{{Cite book |ja|author={{ill|Ueda Mōshin|ja|植田孟縉}}|year=1993|title=武蔵名勝図会 |page= |publisher=慶友社|isbn=4874490808}}</ref>. Since then, it has been protected by the residents of the '''Imokubo area''' as their guardian shrine<ref name="cho"/>.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was granted '''13 koku''' of shrine land ({{ill|Sharyō|ja|社領}})<ref name="city">{{Cite web|ja|title=11 豊鹿島神社|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,5862,360,622,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>.
In '''1993''' ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}} 5), during a major '''restoration of the haiden''' (worship hall), a '''tōsatsu (ridge plaque)''' dated to '''1466''' ({{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正}} 1) was discovered, confirming the shrine's founding year, as previously recorded in the '''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō''' ({{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}). Additionally, five other '''tōsatsu''' were found, revealing that the haiden before the restoration was constructed in '''1858''' ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 5)<ref name="book"/>.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Tokyo Designated Tangible Cultural Property (Architecture) ===
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Honden (Main Hall), 1 building''' - designated as a Tokyo Important Cultural Property on '''November 21, 1964''' (Showa 39), reclassified on '''July 1, 1976'''<ref name="to">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=205|title=豊鹿島神社本殿|website=Tokyo Cultural Property Database|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|ja|東京都教育委員会}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : The shrine features a '''single-bay nagare-zukuri''' ({{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}) main hall with '''kokera-buki (wooden shingles)''' and a '''box ridge'''. The dimensions are '''170.7 cm in width''', with the '''shrine core (moya)''' having a '''depth of 152.4 cm''', and the '''front eaves (kōhai)''' extending '''140.4 cm'''<ref name="to"/>. The structure is currently housed within a '''protective covering hall''' ({{ill|Oudou|ja|覆堂}}) to shield it from the elements, making it inaccessible for direct viewing<ref name="cho"/>. Previously, the '''tōsatsu''' stored inside the main hall was believed to indicate the construction year as '''1550''' ({{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 19), but a '''new tōsatsu''' discovered during the '''1992–1994 dismantling repairs''' confirmed the construction date as '''1466''' (Bunshō 1), with records of a '''master carpenter named Jirōsaburō Chikayoshi'''. The roof was also originally made of '''thick wooden planks (atsuita-buki)'''<ref name="to"/>.
=== Higashiyamato City Designated Cultural Property (Municipal Treasure) ===
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Wooden Komainu (Guardian Lion-Dogs)''' - designated on '''March 1, 1983''' (Showa 58)<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|ja|title=東大和市の文化財|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,2279,359,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : These wooden komainu are believed to have been crafted '''during the same period as the honden'''<ref name="bunka"/>.
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Shishigashira (Lion Head)''' - designated on '''March 1, 1983'''<ref name="bunka"/> : This lion head, likely from the '''late Edo period''', was used in '''lion dances''' ({{ill|Shishimai|ja|獅子舞}}) that were formerly performed at the shrine<ref name="bunka"/>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=Volume 120, Tama District No. 32|chapter=Imokubo Village Kashima Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763994/79}}|publisher={{ill|Imperial Ministry of Home Affairs|ja|内務省地理局}}|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo|ja|東京都の神社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tokyo-designated cultural properties|ja|東京都指定文化財一覧}}

In '''1993''' ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}} 5), during a major '''restoration of the haiden''' (worship hall), a '''tōsatsu (ridge plaque)''' dated to '''1466''' ({{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正}} 1) was discovered, confirming the shrine's founding year, as previously recorded in the '''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō''' ({{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}). Additionally, five other '''tōsatsu''' were found, revealing that the haiden before the restoration was constructed in '''1858''' ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 5)<ref name="book"/>.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Tokyo Designated Tangible Cultural Property (Architecture) ===
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Honden (Main Hall), 1 building''' - designated as a Tokyo Important Cultural Property on '''November 21, 1964''' (Showa 39), reclassified on '''July 1, 1976'''<ref name="to">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=205|title=豊鹿島神社本殿|website=Tokyo Cultural Property Database|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|ja|東京都教育委員会}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : The shrine features a '''single-bay nagare-zukuri''' ({{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}) main hall with '''kokera-buki (wooden shingles)''' and a '''box ridge'''. The dimensions are '''170.7 cm in width''', with the '''shrine core (moya)''' having a '''depth of 152.4 cm''', and the '''front eaves (kōhai)''' extending '''140.4 cm'''<ref name="to"/>. The structure is currently housed within a '''protective covering hall''' ({{ill|Oudou|ja|覆堂}}) to shield it from the elements, making it inaccessible for direct viewing<ref name="cho"/>. Previously, the '''tōsatsu''' stored inside the main hall was believed to indicate the construction year as '''1550''' ({{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 19), but a '''new tōsatsu''' discovered during the '''1992–1994 dismantling repairs''' confirmed the construction date as '''1466''' (Bunshō 1), with records of a '''master carpenter named Jirōsaburō Chikayoshi'''. The roof was also originally made of '''thick wooden planks (atsuita-buki)'''<ref name="to"/>.
=== Higashiyamato City Designated Cultural Property (Municipal Treasure) ===
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Wooden Komainu (Guardian Lion-Dogs)''' - designated on '''March 1, 1983''' (Showa 58)<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|ja|title=東大和市の文化財|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,2279,359,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : These wooden komainu are believed to have been crafted '''during the same period as the honden'''<ref name="bunka"/>.
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Shishigashira (Lion Head)''' - designated on '''March 1, 1983'''<ref name="bunka"/> : This lion head, likely from the '''late Edo period''', was used in '''lion dances''' ({{ill|Shishimai|ja|獅子舞}}) that were formerly performed at the shrine<ref name="bunka"/>.

'''Toyokashima Shrine''' (豊鹿嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyamato, Tokyo|ja|東大和市}}, Tokyo. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御加豆智命}}<ref name="cho">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/higashiyamato/5762/|title=豊鹿嶋神社|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|東京都神社庁}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>. The shrine's '''main hall (honden)''', built in 1466 during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, is the oldest existing {{ill|Shinto shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} in Tokyo<ref name="cho"/>.
== History ==
According to the shrine tradition, in '''707 CE''' ({{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 4), a '''demon god''' that arrived in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}} was pacified at '''Hitachi Peak''', and the shrine was founded by the '''fourth princess of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}''' and {{ill|Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|ja|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂}}<ref name="book">{{Cite book |ja|author={{ill|Ueda Mōshin|ja|植田孟縉}}|year=1993|title=武蔵名勝図会 |page= |publisher=慶友社|isbn=4874490808}}</ref>. Since then, it has been protected by the residents of the '''Imokubo area''' as their guardian shrine<ref name="cho"/>.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was granted '''13 koku''' of shrine land ({{ill|Sharyō|ja|社領}})<ref name="city">{{Cite web|ja|title=11 豊鹿島神社|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,5862,360,622,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>.
In '''1993''' ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}} 5), during a major '''restoration of the haiden''' (worship hall), a '''tōsatsu (ridge plaque)''' dated to '''1466''' ({{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正}} 1) was discovered, confirming the shrine's founding year, as previously recorded in the '''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō''' ({{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}). Additionally, five other '''tōsatsu''' were found, revealing that the haiden before the restoration was constructed in '''1858''' ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 5)<ref name="book"/>.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Tokyo Designated Tangible Cultural Property (Architecture) ===
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Honden (Main Hall), 1 building''' - designated as a Tokyo Important Cultural Property on '''November 21, 1964''' (Showa 39), reclassified on '''July 1, 1976'''<ref name="to">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=205|title=豊鹿島神社本殿|website=Tokyo Cultural Property Database|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|ja|東京都教育委員会}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : The shrine features a '''single-bay nagare-zukuri''' ({{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}) main hall with '''kokera-buki (wooden shingles)''' and a '''box ridge'''. The dimensions are '''170.7 cm in width''', with the '''shrine core (moya)''' having a '''depth of 152.4 cm''', and the '''front eaves (kōhai)''' extending '''140.4 cm'''<ref name="to"/>. The structure is currently housed within a '''protective covering hall''' ({{ill|Oudou|ja|覆堂}}) to shield it from the elements, making it inaccessible for direct viewing<ref name="cho"/>. Previously, the '''tōsatsu''' stored inside the main hall was believed to indicate the construction year as '''1550''' ({{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 19), but a '''new tōsatsu''' discovered during the '''1992–1994 dismantling repairs''' confirmed the construction date as '''1466''' (Bunshō 1), with records of a '''master carpenter named Jirōsaburō Chikayoshi'''. The roof was also originally made of '''thick wooden planks (atsuita-buki)'''<ref name="to"/>.
=== Higashiyamato City Designated Cultural Property (Municipal Treasure) ===
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Wooden Komainu (Guardian Lion-Dogs)''' - designated on '''March 1, 1983''' (Showa 58)<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|ja|title=東大和市の文化財|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,2279,359,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : These wooden komainu are believed to have been crafted '''during the same period as the honden'''<ref name="bunka"/>.
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Shishigashira (Lion Head)''' - designated on '''March 1, 1983'''<ref name="bunka"/> : This lion head, likely from the '''late Edo period''', was used in '''lion dances''' ({{ill|Shishimai|ja|獅子舞}}) that were formerly performed at the shrine<ref name="bunka"/>.

* '''Toyokashima Shrine Wooden Komainu (Guardian Lion-Dogs)''' - designated on '''March 1, 1983''' (Showa 58)<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|ja|title=東大和市の文化財|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,2279,359,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : These wooden komainu are believed to have been crafted '''during the same period as the honden'''<ref name="bunka"/>.
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Shishigashira (Lion Head)''' - designated on '''March 1, 1983'''<ref name="bunka"/> : This lion head, likely from the '''late Edo period''', was used in '''lion dances''' ({{ill|Shishimai|ja|獅子舞}}) that were formerly performed at the shrine<ref name="bunka"/>.

== Bibliography == * {{cite book|ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=Volume 120, Tama District No. 32|chapter=Imokubo Village Kashima Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763994/79}}|publisher={{ill|Imperial Ministry of Home Affairs|ja|内務省地理局}}|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō}}}}

revision.text:
{{表記揺れ案内|表記1=豊鹿嶋神社|表記2=豊鹿島神社|表記3=|議論ページ=}} {{Shrine | Name = Toyokashima Shrine | image = [[File:豊鹿嶋神社正面より.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Higashiyamato, Tokyo|ja|東大和市}} Imokubo 1-2067<ref name="cho"/> | coordinates = {{Coord|35|45|28.5|N|139|24|46.2|E|type:landmark_region:JP-13}} | deity = {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御加豆智命}} | Status = {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} | Established = 1466 | Festival = | Map = Japan Tokyo }}
'''Toyokashima Shrine''' (豊鹿嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyamato, Tokyo|ja|東大和市}}, Tokyo. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御加豆智命}}<ref name="cho">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/higashiyamato/5762/|title=豊鹿嶋神社|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|東京都神社庁}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>. The shrine's '''main hall (honden)''', built in 1466 during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, is the oldest existing {{ill|Shinto shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} in Tokyo<ref name="cho"/>.
== History ==
According to the shrine tradition, in '''707 CE''' ({{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 4), a '''demon god''' that arrived in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}} was pacified at '''Hitachi Peak''', and the shrine was founded by the '''fourth princess of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}''' and {{ill|Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|ja|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂}}<ref name="book">{{Cite book |ja|author={{ill|Ueda Mōshin|ja|植田孟縉}}|year=1993|title=武蔵名勝図会 |page= |publisher=慶友社|isbn=4874490808}}</ref>. Since then, it has been protected by the residents of the '''Imokubo area''' as their guardian shrine<ref name="cho"/>.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was granted '''13 koku''' of shrine land ({{ill|Sharyō|ja|社領}})<ref name="city">{{Cite web|ja|title=11 豊鹿島神社|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,5862,360,622,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>.
In '''1993''' ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}} 5), during a major '''restoration of the haiden''' (worship hall), a '''tōsatsu (ridge plaque)''' dated to '''1466''' ({{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正}} 1) was discovered, confirming the shrine's founding year, as previously recorded in the '''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō''' ({{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}). Additionally, five other '''tōsatsu''' were found, revealing that the haiden before the restoration was constructed in '''1858''' ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 5)<ref name="book"/>.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Tokyo Designated Tangible Cultural Property (Architecture) ===
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Honden (Main Hall), 1 building''' - designated as a Tokyo Important Cultural Property on '''November 21, 1964''' (Showa 39), reclassified on '''July 1, 1976'''<ref name="to">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=205|title=豊鹿島神社本殿|website=Tokyo Cultural Property Database|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|ja|東京都教育委員会}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : The shrine features a '''single-bay nagare-zukuri''' ({{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}) main hall with '''kokera-buki (wooden shingles)''' and a '''box ridge'''. The dimensions are '''170.7 cm in width''', with the '''shrine core (moya)''' having a '''depth of 152.4 cm''', and the '''front eaves (kōhai)''' extending '''140.4 cm'''<ref name="to"/>. The structure is currently housed within a '''protective covering hall''' ({{ill|Oudou|ja|覆堂}}) to shield it from the elements, making it inaccessible for direct viewing<ref name="cho"/>. Previously, the '''tōsatsu''' stored inside the main hall was believed to indicate the construction year as '''1550''' ({{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 19), but a '''new tōsatsu''' discovered during the '''1992–1994 dismantling repairs''' confirmed the construction date as '''1466''' (Bunshō 1), with records of a '''master carpenter named Jirōsaburō Chikayoshi'''. The roof was also originally made of '''thick wooden planks (atsuita-buki)'''<ref name="to"/>.
=== Higashiyamato City Designated Cultural Property (Municipal Treasure) ===
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Wooden Komainu (Guardian Lion-Dogs)''' - designated on '''March 1, 1983''' (Showa 58)<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|ja|title=東大和市の文化財|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,2279,359,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : These wooden komainu are believed to have been crafted '''during the same period as the honden'''<ref name="bunka"/>.
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Shishigashira (Lion Head)''' - designated on '''March 1, 1983'''<ref name="bunka"/> : This lion head, likely from the '''late Edo period''', was used in '''lion dances''' ({{ill|Shishimai|ja|獅子舞}}) that were formerly performed at the shrine<ref name="bunka"/>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=Volume 120, Tama District No. 32|chapter=Imokubo Village Kashima Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763994/79}}|publisher={{ill|Imperial Ministry of Home Affairs|ja|内務省地理局}}|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo|ja|東京都の神社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tokyo-designated cultural properties|ja|東京都指定文化財一覧}}

{{表記揺れ案内|表記1=豊鹿嶋神社|表記2=豊鹿島神社|表記3=|議論ページ=}} {{Shrine | Name = Toyokashima Shrine | image = [[File:豊鹿嶋神社正面より.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Higashiyamato, Tokyo|ja|東大和市}} Imokubo 1-2067<ref name="cho"/> | coordinates = {{Coord|35|45|28.5|N|139|24|46.2|E|type:landmark_region:JP-13}} | deity = {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御加豆智命}} | Status = {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} | Established = 1466 | Festival = | Map = Japan Tokyo }}
'''Toyokashima Shrine''' (豊鹿嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyamato, Tokyo|ja|東大和市}}, Tokyo. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御加豆智命}}<ref name="cho">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/higashiyamato/5762/|title=豊鹿嶋神社|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|東京都神社庁}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>. The shrine's '''main hall (honden)''', built in 1466 during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, is the oldest existing {{ill|Shinto shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} in Tokyo<ref name="cho"/>.
== History ==
According to the shrine tradition, in '''707 CE''' ({{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 4), a '''demon god''' that arrived in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}} was pacified at '''Hitachi Peak''', and the shrine was founded by the '''fourth princess of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}''' and {{ill|Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|ja|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂}}<ref name="book">{{Cite book |ja|author={{ill|Ueda Mōshin|ja|植田孟縉}}|year=1993|title=武蔵名勝図会 |page= |publisher=慶友社|isbn=4874490808}}</ref>. Since then, it has been protected by the residents of the '''Imokubo area''' as their guardian shrine<ref name="cho"/>.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was granted '''13 koku''' of shrine land ({{ill|Sharyō|ja|社領}})<ref name="city">{{Cite web|ja|title=11 豊鹿島神社|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,5862,360,622,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>.
In '''1993''' ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}} 5), during a major '''restoration of the haiden''' (worship hall), a '''tōsatsu (ridge plaque)''' dated to '''1466''' ({{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正}} 1) was discovered, confirming the shrine's founding year, as previously recorded in the '''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō''' ({{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}). Additionally, five other '''tōsatsu''' were found, revealing that the haiden before the restoration was constructed in '''1858''' ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 5)<ref name="book"/>.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Tokyo Designated Tangible Cultural Property (Architecture) ===
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Honden (Main Hall), 1 building''' - designated as a Tokyo Important Cultural Property on '''November 21, 1964''' (Showa 39), reclassified on '''July 1, 1976'''<ref name="to">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=205|title=豊鹿島神社本殿|website=Tokyo Cultural Property Database|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|ja|東京都教育委員会}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : The shrine features a '''single-bay nagare-zukuri''' ({{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}) main hall with '''kokera-buki (wooden shingles)''' and a '''box ridge'''. The dimensions are '''170.7 cm in width''', with the '''shrine core (moya)''' having a '''depth of 152.4 cm''', and the '''front eaves (kōhai)''' extending '''140.4 cm'''<ref name="to"/>. The structure is currently housed within a '''protective covering hall''' ({{ill|Oudou|ja|覆堂}}) to shield it from the elements, making it inaccessible for direct viewing<ref name="cho"/>. Previously, the '''tōsatsu''' stored inside the main hall was believed to indicate the construction year as '''1550''' ({{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 19), but a '''new tōsatsu''' discovered during the '''1992–1994 dismantling repairs''' confirmed the construction date as '''1466''' (Bunshō 1), with records of a '''master carpenter named Jirōsaburō Chikayoshi'''. The roof was also originally made of '''thick wooden planks (atsuita-buki)'''<ref name="to"/>.
=== Higashiyamato City Designated Cultural Property (Municipal Treasure) ===
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Wooden Komainu (Guardian Lion-Dogs)''' - designated on '''March 1, 1983''' (Showa 58)<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|ja|title=東大和市の文化財|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,2279,359,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : These wooden komainu are believed to have been crafted '''during the same period as the honden'''<ref name="bunka"/>.
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Shishigashira (Lion Head)''' - designated on '''March 1, 1983'''<ref name="bunka"/> : This lion head, likely from the '''late Edo period''', was used in '''lion dances''' ({{ill|Shishimai|ja|獅子舞}}) that were formerly performed at the shrine<ref name="bunka"/>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=Volume 120, Tama District No. 32|chapter=Imokubo Village Kashima Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763994/79}}|publisher={{ill|Imperial Ministry of Home Affairs|ja|内務省地理局}}|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō}}}}

According to the shrine tradition, in '''707 CE''' ({{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 4), a '''demon god''' that arrived in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}} was pacified at '''Hitachi Peak''', and the shrine was founded by the '''fourth princess of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}''' and {{ill|Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|ja|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂}}<ref name="book">{{Cite book |ja|author={{ill|Ueda Mōshin|ja|植田孟縉}}|year=1993|title=武蔵名勝図会 |page= |publisher=慶友社|isbn=4874490808}}</ref>. Since then, it has been protected by the residents of the '''Imokubo area''' as their guardian shrine<ref name="cho"/>.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was granted '''13 koku''' of shrine land ({{ill|Sharyō|ja|社領}})<ref name="city">{{Cite web|ja|title=11 豊鹿島神社|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,5862,360,622,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>.
In '''1993''' ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}} 5), during a major '''restoration of the haiden''' (worship hall), a '''tōsatsu (ridge plaque)''' dated to '''1466''' ({{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正}} 1) was discovered, confirming the shrine's founding year, as previously recorded in the '''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō''' ({{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}). Additionally, five other '''tōsatsu''' were found, revealing that the haiden before the restoration was constructed in '''1858''' ({{ill|Ansei|ja|安政}} 5)<ref name="book"/>.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Tokyo Designated Tangible Cultural Property (Architecture) ===
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Honden (Main Hall), 1 building''' - designated as a Tokyo Important Cultural Property on '''November 21, 1964''' (Showa 39), reclassified on '''July 1, 1976'''<ref name="to">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=205|title=豊鹿島神社本殿|website=Tokyo Cultural Property Database|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|ja|東京都教育委員会}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : The shrine features a '''single-bay nagare-zukuri''' ({{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}) main hall with '''kokera-buki (wooden shingles)''' and a '''box ridge'''. The dimensions are '''170.7 cm in width''', with the '''shrine core (moya)''' having a '''depth of 152.4 cm''', and the '''front eaves (kōhai)''' extending '''140.4 cm'''<ref name="to"/>. The structure is currently housed within a '''protective covering hall''' ({{ill|Oudou|ja|覆堂}}) to shield it from the elements, making it inaccessible for direct viewing<ref name="cho"/>. Previously, the '''tōsatsu''' stored inside the main hall was believed to indicate the construction year as '''1550''' ({{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 19), but a '''new tōsatsu''' discovered during the '''1992–1994 dismantling repairs''' confirmed the construction date as '''1466''' (Bunshō 1), with records of a '''master carpenter named Jirōsaburō Chikayoshi'''. The roof was also originally made of '''thick wooden planks (atsuita-buki)'''<ref name="to"/>.
=== Higashiyamato City Designated Cultural Property (Municipal Treasure) ===
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Wooden Komainu (Guardian Lion-Dogs)''' - designated on '''March 1, 1983''' (Showa 58)<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|ja|title=東大和市の文化財|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,2279,359,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : These wooden komainu are believed to have been crafted '''during the same period as the honden'''<ref name="bunka"/>.
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Shishigashira (Lion Head)''' - designated on '''March 1, 1983'''<ref name="bunka"/> : This lion head, likely from the '''late Edo period''', was used in '''lion dances''' ({{ill|Shishimai|ja|獅子舞}}) that were formerly performed at the shrine<ref name="bunka"/>.

'''Toyokashima Shrine''' (豊鹿嶋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyamato, Tokyo|ja|東大和市}}, Tokyo. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御加豆智命}}<ref name="cho">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/higashiyamato/5762/|title=豊鹿嶋神社|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|東京都神社庁}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>. The shrine's '''main hall (honden)''', built in 1466 during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, is the oldest existing {{ill|Shinto shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} in Tokyo<ref name="cho"/>.
== History ==
According to the shrine tradition, in '''707 CE''' ({{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} 4), a '''demon god''' that arrived in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}} was pacified at '''Hitachi Peak''', and the shrine was founded by the '''fourth princess of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}''' and {{ill|Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|ja|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂}}<ref name="book">{{Cite book |ja|author={{ill|Ueda Mōshin|ja|植田孟縉}}|year=1993|title=武蔵名勝図会 |page= |publisher=慶友社|isbn=4874490808}}</ref>. Since then, it has been protected by the residents of the '''Imokubo area''' as their guardian shrine<ref name="cho"/>.
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was granted '''13 koku''' of shrine land ({{ill|Sharyō|ja|社領}})<ref name="city">{{Cite web|ja|title=11 豊鹿島神社|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,5862,360,622,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-27}}</ref>.
In '''1993''' ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}} 5), during a major '''restoration of the haiden''' (worship hall), a '''tōsatsu (ridge plaque)''' dated to '''1466''' ({{ill|Bunshō|ja|文正}} 1) was discovered, confirming the shrine's founding year, as previously recorded in the '''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō''' ({{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}). The roof was also originally made of '''thick wooden planks (atsuita-buki)'''<ref name="to"/>.

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Tokyo Designated Tangible Cultural Property (Architecture) ===
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Honden (Main Hall), 1 building''' - designated as a Tokyo Important Cultural Property on '''November 21, 1964''' (Showa 39), reclassified on '''July 1, 1976'''<ref name="to">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=205|title=豊鹿島神社本殿|website=Tokyo Cultural Property Database|publisher={{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|ja|東京都教育委員会}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : The shrine features a '''single-bay nagare-zukuri''' ({{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}) main hall with '''kokera-buki (wooden shingles)''' and a '''box ridge'''. The dimensions are '''170.7 cm in width''', with the '''shrine core (moya)''' having a '''depth of 152.4 cm''', and the '''front eaves (kōhai)''' extending '''140.4 cm'''<ref name="to"/>. The structure is currently housed within a '''protective covering hall''' ({{ill|Oudou|ja|覆堂}}) to shield it from the elements, making it inaccessible for direct viewing<ref name="cho"/>. Previously, the '''tōsatsu''' stored inside the main hall was believed to indicate the construction year as '''1550''' ({{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 19), but a '''new tōsatsu''' discovered during the '''1992–1994 dismantling repairs''' confirmed the construction date as '''1466''' (Bunshō 1), with records of a '''master carpenter named Jirōsaburō Chikayoshi'''. The roof was also originally made of '''thick wooden planks (atsuita-buki)'''<ref name="to"/>.
=== Higashiyamato City Designated Cultural Property (Municipal Treasure) ===
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Wooden Komainu (Guardian Lion-Dogs)''' - designated on '''March 1, 1983''' (Showa 58)<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|ja|title=東大和市の文化財|url=https://www.city.higashiyamato.lg.jp/index.cfm/34,2279,359,html|publisher={{ill|Higashiyamato City|ja|東大和市}}|accessdate=2019-09-28}}</ref> : These wooden komainu are believed to have been crafted '''during the same period as the honden'''<ref name="bunka"/>.
* '''Toyokashima Shrine Shishigashira (Lion Head)''' - designated on '''March 1, 1983'''<ref name="bunka"/> : This lion head, likely from the '''late Edo period''', was used in '''lion dances''' ({{ill|Shishimai|ja|獅子舞}}) that were formerly performed at the shrine<ref name="bunka"/>.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=Volume 120, Tama District No. 32|chapter=Imokubo Village Kashima Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763994/79}}|publisher={{ill|Imperial Ministry of Home Affairs|ja|内務省地理局}}|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō}}}} == See Also! == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo|ja|東京都の神社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tokyo-designated cultural properties|ja|東京都指定文化財一覧}}
